title: Best VPN for Switzerland in 2026
description: The best VPNs for Switzerland in 2026. VPN legality under Swiss law, data retention requirements, top picks for privacy and streaming (SRF, Netflix), and how Swiss nFADP affects your VPN choice.
author: VPN Adviser Editorial

mainEntity.name: Is it legal to use a VPN in Switzerland?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, VPN use is legal in Switzerland for personal use. Switzerland is not an EU member and has its own data protection laws (the revised Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection, nFADP, in effect since 2023), which are generally strong by international standards. Using a VPN to protect your privacy, secure public Wi-Fi connections, or access geo-restricted content is legal. However, using a VPN to conduct illegal activities remains illegal regardless of whether a VPN is in use. Switzerland participates in international law enforcement cooperation, so serious criminal activity is not shielded by Swiss VPN use.

mainEntity.name: Which VPN works best for Switzerland in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The best VPNs for Switzerland in 2026 are: (1) NordVPN -- fast Swiss servers, strict no-logs policy audited by Deloitte, excellent streaming unblocking for SRF and international services, and strong privacy protection; (2) ExpressVPN -- reliable performance from Swiss servers, works with most streaming platforms, and a proven no-logs policy; (3) Mullvad -- ideal for maximum anonymity, accepts anonymous payment, no email required to sign up, based in Sweden with strict no-logs policy; (4) ProtonVPN -- Swiss-based provider with strong privacy credentials, headquartered in Geneva, and open-source apps. For most users balancing speed, streaming, and privacy, NordVPN or ProtonVPN are the top choices. Mullvad is best for users prioritizing anonymity above all else.

mainEntity.name: Does Switzerland have data retention laws that affect VPN privacy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Switzerland requires telecom providers and internet service providers to retain connection metadata (who connected to what, when, for how long) for 6 months under the Federal Act on the Surveillance of Post and Telecommunications (BUPF). This applies to ISPs and telecoms, not necessarily to VPN providers operating solely in Switzerland. However, if a VPN provider is registered in Switzerland and classified as a telecommunications provider, they could be subject to these requirements. ProtonVPN, based in Geneva, has argued they are not subject to BUPF as a VPN provider and have published transparency reports showing minimal data retention. For users concerned about this distinction, choosing a Switzerland-based VPN with a verified no-logs policy (confirmed by independent audit) or a VPN based outside Switzerland are both reasonable approaches.

content:
Switzerland sits outside the EU, runs its own data protection regime, and is home to several privacy-focused technology companies including ProtonMail and Proton VPN. It is also a country with above-average surveillance infrastructure under the BUPF law. This combination makes VPN choice in Switzerland more nuanced than in most European countries.

VPN Legality in Switzerland

Using a VPN is legal in Switzerland. The revised Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP), which came into force in September 2023, strengthened individual privacy rights and increased penalties for data breaches, but does not restrict VPN use. Swiss law enforcement does cooperate internationally on serious criminal matters, but personal VPN use for privacy, security on public Wi-Fi, or accessing geo-restricted content raises no legal issues.

Switzerland's Surveillance Laws: What VPN Users Should Know

The Federal Act on the Surveillance of Post and Telecommunications (BUPF) requires Swiss telecom and ISP providers to retain connection metadata for 6 months. This covers which IP address connected to which service, when, and for how long. It does not cover content of communications. The practical implication for VPN users: your ISP will see that you connected to a VPN server, but not what you did through the VPN. A VPN provider operating in Switzerland may or may not be classified as a telecommunications provider subject to BUPF. ProtonVPN has publicly stated they are not subject to BUPF in their capacity as a VPN service and publishes regular transparency reports.

Best VPNs for Switzerland in 2026

NordVPN

NordVPN maintains multiple servers in Switzerland and delivers consistently fast speeds for both domestic and international connections. The no-logs policy has been audited by Deloitte and PricewaterhouseCoopers. For users who want to access SRF Play or Swiss regional content while traveling, NordVPN's Swiss servers work reliably. The service also unblocks Netflix, Disney+, and other streaming platforms from Swiss IP addresses. NordVPN is based in Panama, outside EU and Swiss jurisdiction, which simplifies the data retention question.

ProtonVPN

ProtonVPN is headquartered in Geneva and founded by CERN scientists. Its location in Switzerland is both a strength (strong Swiss privacy law) and a point to understand (potential applicability of BUPF, which Proton argues does not apply to their service category). Proton publishes transparency reports showing the rare instances where authorities have made requests and what data, if any, was provided. For users who value the credibility of a Swiss privacy-tech brand with a strong public track record, ProtonVPN is a compelling choice. The free tier is among the most generous of any VPN service.

Mullvad

Mullvad is based in Sweden (outside Switzerland but with strong privacy laws) and requires no email address to sign up. You receive an account number and can pay with cash, cryptocurrency, or standard payment methods. Mullvad has demonstrated its no-logs policy in practice: when Swedish police raided a Mullvad data center in 2023, they left with no user data because none was stored. For users for whom anonymity is the primary concern above streaming or ease of use, Mullvad is the strongest option available.

ExpressVPN

ExpressVPN offers fast Swiss servers and works with most major streaming platforms. Its no-logs policy was verified when Turkish authorities seized an ExpressVPN server in 2017 and found no user activity data. Based in the British Virgin Islands, ExpressVPN operates outside EU and Swiss jurisdiction. It is a strong all-around choice for users who want reliable performance without configuring anything.

VPN for Streaming Swiss Content

SRF Play (Swiss public broadcaster) restricts content to Swiss IP addresses. A VPN with Swiss servers solves this when traveling abroad. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and ProtonVPN all have Swiss servers that work with SRF Play. Check that the VPN you choose lists Switzerland as a server location before subscribing specifically for this use case.

Speed Considerations

Switzerland has among the fastest average internet speeds in Europe. This means VPN overhead is less noticeable here than in many countries. Any of the top-tier providers listed above will deliver speeds adequate for 4K streaming and low-latency applications. For maximum speed, choose a provider with multiple Swiss server locations so you can connect to the nearest one.
